channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New bill proposes abolishing all property taxes in Pennsylvania
item.description: The bill, proposed by Rep. Russ Diamond (R-Lebanon) would "abolish all property taxes in the Commonwealth as of July 1, 2030.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders creation of US sovereign wealth fund, says it could own part of TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday signed an executive order directing the U.S. to take steps to start developing a government-owned investment fund that he said could be used to profit off of TikTok if he's successful at finding it an American buyer.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico, but import taxes still in place for Canada and China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump held off Monday on his tariff threats against Mexico for one month of further negotiations after Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum agreed to send 10,000 members of her country's national guard to the border to address drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Transportation secretary says 'expansive' impact zone was left by Philadelphia medical plane crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The impact area where a medical transportation plane crashed in Philadelphia was notably “expansive,” U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Monday after viewing the site with investigators and city officials. Duffy said he was struck by “how expansive it was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic state Sen. Mallory McMorrow preparing to enter US Senate race in Michigan, AP sources say
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan state Sen. Mallory McMorrow is preparing to enter the Democratic primary for an open U.S. Senate seat in the state, multiple people familiar with her plans confirmed on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Thousands protest in St. Louis over federal immigration policies
item.description: The protest, which saw over 1,000 participants, was in response to recent actions by the Trump administration that suspended numerous immigration initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, NewsNation

item.title: Block on Trump administration federal funding freeze will likely be extended, Washington judge says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge in the nation's capital will likely keep a temporary block on a Trump administration plan for a freeze on federal funding after lawyers for nonprofit groups said they're still struggling to get promised grants and loans. U.S. District Judge Loren L.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: L.A. filmmakers win Doritos ad contest, land Super Bowl commerical
item.description: Two Los Angeles filmmakers will have their commercial air during Super Bowl LIX after winning Doritos' "Crash the Super Bowl Contest." The winning duo, Dylan Bradshaw and Nate Norell, are best friends and co-directors of the ad. Bradshaw moved to the L.A.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's threat of tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is aimed at stopping the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. Ingredients for fentanyl are produced in China and used by pharmaceutical companies to make legal painkillers.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What is known about the deadly collision between a passenger jet and Army helicopter
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all 67 aboard in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk is a 'special government employee,' the White House confirms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is working for President Donald Trump as a “special government employee," according to a White House official, solidifying his controversial role in the administration but sidestepping some disclosure rules that are typical of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly a month after devastating Southern California wildfires
item.description: The Pacific Coast Highway is back open in Malibu, California, nearly a month after the deadly Palisades Fire ripped through hillside Los Angeles neighborhoods, destroying properties all the way down to the beach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The city of Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to a woman who alleged that former policeman Derek Chauvin pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just months before he used the same maneuver on George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fort Bliss sergeant helps lead Army's fight against cancer
item.description: “The Army represents the opportunity to double or triple the amount of people on the (national bone marrow registry) each year,” Sutton said.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Baltazar, NewsNation

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lady Gaga defends transgender community during Grammys speech
item.description: "The trans community deserves to be lifted up," Lady Gaga, who was born Stefani Germanotta, said to applause.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Driver falls asleep in car, wakes up inside Waffle House
item.description: A driver told police he fell asleep at the wheel and woke up inside a Waffle House in northeast Memphis.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Man hit in head by debris from Philadelphia plane crash that broke through diner window
item.description: A Philadelphia diner patron was injured Friday when a piece of debris from a medevac jet that crashed hurtled through a window and hit the man in the head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans sued over Bourbon Street terror attack, accused of negligence that cost lives
item.description: New Orleans terrorist attack victims and survivors have filed a civil lawsuit against city leaders for negligence after Shamsud-Din Jabbar drove his truck into Bourbon Street crowds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: Deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: NTSB releases video of responders searching through eerie site of fatal Philadelphia Learjet crash
item.description: The NTSB published video showing officials at the site of the fatal Jan. 31 plane crash in Philadelphia, which killed at least seven people and injured 22 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter wins posthumous Grammy for narrating audiobook
item.description: If the former president won before his death, he would've become the oldest Grammy award winner in history.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Flight headed to LaGuardia evacuated after engine issue: FAA
item.description: Passengers were taken back to the terminal after deplaning via slides and stairs, according to a spokesperson for United Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, KRON4

item.title: Video shows wing of United Airlines plane catching fire during takeoff at Houston airport
item.description: Wild video captures the moment a United Airlines flight from Houston to New York was delayed after a wing on the plane caught fire during takeoff on Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Trump’s tariffs targeting Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: President Donald Trump has taken executive action to impose or threaten new tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico and China.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Airport employee injured in collision with aircraft wing at Chicago's O'Hare
item.description: The driver of a tug vehicle at Chicago O'Hare International Airport was injured Saturday evening after colliding with the wing of an airplane.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan priest has license revoked after imitating Musk's gesture at Pro-Life Summit
item.description: The priest, Calvin Robinson, attended the National Pro-Life Summit in Washington, D.C. last month. Toward the end of his remarks at the Jan. 25 event, he mimicked Musk’s salute, which made the rounds online.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Trump aid freeze disrupts global HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: A legacy bipartisan initiative to combat HIV and AIDS in Africa is collateral damage from President Donald Trump’s directive to halt all U.S. foreign assistance, despite efforts to exempt humanitarian assistance and lifesaving medication from being caught up in the three-month funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9
